text: We need the system to send emails to customers and vendors as quickly as possible — ideally in less than a second.

text: We need the system to handle a minimum of 1000 concurrent users without any performance degradation.
context_text: We need the system to handle a minimum of 1000 concurrent users without any performance degradation. For our e-commerce platform, it should be able to process transactions quickly — ideally within less than 2 seconds from when a customer clicks 'buy now'.
turn_text: We need the system to handle a minimum of 1000 concurrent users without any performance degradation. For our e-commerce platform, it should be able to process transactions quickly — ideally within less than 2 seconds from when a customer clicks 'buy now'. Security is a big concern for us; customer data needs to be encrypted both in transit and at rest. We've had issues with slow login times, so we need the system to authenticate users efficiently, ensuring that it never takes more than 5 seconds for a user to log in. For returning customers, the system should remember their card details but verify the card is still valid upon each login. It's important to us because we want to reduce friction and increase customer satisfaction. Additionally, the system must support multiple browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge on both Windows and macOS operating systems. We need it to be compatible with our existing server infrastructure, specifically using Apache for web servers and Nginx for reverse proxying. The user interface should display product titles and metadata prominently but never show any private information like credit card numbers or passwords in the browser interface. Only authorized users, identified by their unique login credentials, should have access to certain administrative features. We must not allow unauthorized users to modify or view sensitive data. Lastly, we need robust logging and auditing capabilities for all user activities, ensuring that every action is recorded and can be traced back to a specific user.

text: Security is a top priority for us.
context_text: Security is a top priority for us. To protect the system, we need it to be safeguarded by an open-source firewall called Firestarter.
turn_text: Security is a top priority for us. To protect the system, we need it to be safeguarded by an open-source firewall called Firestarter. Additionally, the system should automatically detect and block IP addresses that show signs of DOS attacks. We also want all sensitive information to be encrypted using https to prevent any breaches. Lastly, we need the system to monitor login attempts; if someone tries to log in multiple times without success, it should lock them out after a certain number of failed attempts.

Lastly, we need the system to monitor login attempts; if someone tries to log in multiple times without success, it should lock them out after a certain number of failed attempts.

text: During peak usage times, such as when we have a large number of concurrent users, the system should be able to handle searches efficiently.
context_text: During peak usage times, such as when we have a large number of concurrent users, the system should be able to handle searches efficiently. For example, it should use Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm for searching, and it needs to perform these searches quickly — ideally in less than 1 second per search.
turn_text: During peak usage times, such as when we have a large number of concurrent users, the system should be able to handle searches efficiently. For example, it should use Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm for searching, and it needs to perform these searches quickly — ideally in less than 1 second per search. Additionally, when dealing with a high volume of data, like restoring records or retrieving products, we need the system to maintain its speed. Specifically, it should be able to restore 1000 records per second and retrieve up to 200 products per second without any significant slowdowns.

text: We need the system to be able to run test shipping orders regularly.
context_text: We need the system to be able to run test shipping orders regularly. Ideally it would happen automatically at least once a day, but we should also have the option to manually trigger these tests whenever necessary.
turn_text: We need the system to be able to run test shipping orders regularly. Ideally it would happen automatically at least once a day, but we should also have the option to manually trigger these tests whenever necessary. The types of tests include verifying that all order information is correctly processed and that the shipping details are accurate. It's important because we want to ensure everything works smoothly before actual customer orders go through. We've had issues with incorrect shipping in the past, so frequent testing helps catch those problems early.

text: For sure, we need the system to send out automated test emails during scheduled maintenance and after software updates.
context_text: For sure, we need the system to send out automated test emails during scheduled maintenance and after software updates. This is important because it ensures that our email functionality remains reliable.
turn_text: For sure, we need the system to send out automated test emails during scheduled maintenance and after software updates. This is important because it ensures that our email functionality remains reliable. During maintenance windows, say between 8 PM and 2 AM on Tuesday nights, the system should automatically check if all outgoing emails are working properly. Similarly, right after a new version of the software is deployed, we want to make sure everything is still functioning as expected, so the system should send out test emails then too. This way, any issues can be caught early and addressed before they affect our users.

text: Ideally it would initiate a backup during off-peak hours to minimize any impact on our users.
context_text: Ideally it would initiate a backup during off-peak hours to minimize any impact on our users. Specifically, the system should start backing up critical data between 2 AM and 6 AM local time.
turn_text: Ideally it would initiate a backup during off-peak hours to minimize any impact on our users. Specifically, the system should start backing up critical data between 2 AM and 6 AM local time. This is important because we have had issues with backups interfering with user experience during regular business hours. Additionally, before performing maintenance tasks such as software updates or server checks, it should complete a backup to ensure that any changes are safe and can be reverted if necessary. We need this to happen automatically so our IT team doesn't have to manually trigger the process every time.
